title: Prepare and separately authorize a fresh pair after executable hardening
goal: After T002G is merged and verified and provider availability is no longer known to be blocked, create entirely new requests, bindings, output paths and reviewed one-shot authorization for exactly one baseline and one treatment process.

approval.note: Registration does not authorize execution. A new reviewed authorization after T002G must bind a fresh pair and must not run while provider/session availability is known to be blocked.

acceptance.assertion: No T002D or T002F authorization, pair, ledger, intent, output path, report or receipt is reused.
Known provider or session-limit unavailability blocks before authorization and provider intent; no speculative attempt is consumed while availability is known false.
A separately reviewed one-shot authorization binds exact post-T002G code, executable identity, credential commitment, requests, manifests, sources and hard cost ceilings.
At most one baseline and one treatment process may be attempted; any failure or ambiguity blocks without retry.

metadata.source_observation: Claude CLI reported that the session limit resets at 21:50 Europe/Berlin during the separate Symbol-Call-Graph coding job.
does_not_authorize: a live pair before separate review
